purpose: Aggregate of dual paraboloid filter (DPF) experiments. The DPF is mathematically equivalent to the existing row Groebner system after eliminating auxiliary c_i. We record GB results from running row equations augmented with the column-Plucker (c_i-eliminated) Plucker polynomials. The findings are: (1) all 15 n=8 survivors give the same GB result as the existing row-only attack; (2) all 16 n=9 Groebner families give the same result; (3) cyclic n=10 patterns are killed by the existing vertex-circle order filter, redundant to GB; (4) cyclic n=11 patterns are killed by vertex-circle order filter so GB is not needed. NO new exact obstruction is produced beyond the existing toolkit.

n8_results.purpose: Compare full dual paraboloid filter vs column-only Plucker subsystem on registered finite-case incidence patterns. The full DPF is mathematically equivalent to the existing row Groebner system after eliminating auxiliary c_i; the column-only run is a weaker subsystem and reports only those patterns whose Plucker column determinants alone form an infeasible polynomial system.

n9_results.purpose: Compare full dual paraboloid filter vs column-only Plucker subsystem on registered finite-case incidence patterns. The full DPF is mathematically equivalent to the existing row Groebner system after eliminating auxiliary c_i; the column-only run is a weaker subsystem and reports only those patterns whose Plucker column determinants alone form an infeasible polynomial system.

n11_cyclic.note: GB not run; vcof already kills all 4 patterns in natural order
